2605.27284.title: FineVLA: Fine-Grained Instruction Alignment for Steerable Vision-Language-Action Policies
2605.27284.paper_summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are increasingly expected to not only complete robot tasks, but also follow human instructions about how those tasks should be executed. However, existing robot datasets usually pair trajectories with coarse goal-level language, leaving execution-critical details such as active arm, approach direction, and contact region unspecified. This limits steerable policy learning and robotic video understanding. We introduce FineVLA, an open framework for action-aligned fine-grained VLA supervision. The framework includes: (1) a data construction tool that unifies 972,247 trajectories across 85K tasks from 10 open-source robot datasets and builds FineVLA-Data, a human-verified dataset of 47,159 fine-grained trajectories; (2) a held-out benchmark with 500 videos, 10,816 atomic facts, and 1,030 VQA questions; (3) a robotics-specialized VLM annotator for scalable fine-grained annotation; and (4) a steerable VLA policy trained with controlled mixtures of fine-grained and raw goal-level instructions. Our experiments yield three findings. First, fine-grained supervision does not sacrifice goal-level success: FG-only improves over Raw-only by +1.4 to +8.1 success-rate points across settings. Second, fine-grained and raw instructions are complementary, following a consistent inverted-U trend peaking at FG:Raw = 1:2 to 1:1. The best mixed setting reaches 86.8%/82.5% in RoboTwin simulation and 62.7/100 in real-world dual-arm manipulation (vs. 49.9 Raw-only). Third, fine-grained supervision improves steerable control: the largest real-world gains appear on pose (+23), color (+18), and approach direction (+18)--factors where goal-level instructions provide no guidance. Overall, fine-grained language should augment goal-level instructions: specifying how to execute alongside what to achieve. Project page: https://finevla.xlang.ai/
2605.27284.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型越來越被期望不僅能完成機器人任務，還能遵循人類關於如何執行這些任務的指示。<lb/>然而，現有的機器人數據集通常將軌跡與粗略的目標級語言配對，留下執行關鍵的細節，如主動手臂、接近方向和接觸區域未被指定。<lb/>這限制了可引導的策略學習和機器人視頻理解。<lb/>我們引入了FineVLA，一個用於行動對齊的細粒度VLA監督的開放框架。<lb/>該框架包括：（1）一個數據構建工具，統一了來自10個開源機器人數據集的972,247條軌跡，並構建了FineVLA-Data，一個經人類驗證的47,159條細粒度軌跡數據集；<lb/>（2）一個包含500個視頻、10,816個原子事實和1,030個VQA問題的保留基準；<lb/>（3）一個專門針對機器人的VLM標註工具，用於可擴展的細粒度標註；<lb/>以及（4）一個使用細粒度和原始目標級指示的受控混合訓練的可引導VLA策略。<lb/>我們的實驗得出了三個發現。<lb/>首先，細粒度監督不會犧牲目標級成功率：僅FG在各種設置中比僅Raw提高了+1.4到+8.1的成功率點。<lb/>其次，細粒度和原始指示是互補的，遵循一致的倒U形趨勢，在FG:Raw = 1:2到1:1之間達到峰值。<lb/>最佳混合設置在RoboTwin模擬中達到86.8%/82.5%，在現實世界的雙臂操作中達到62.7/100（相比於49.9的僅Raw）。<lb/>第三，細粒度監督改善了可引導控制：最大的現實世界增益出現在姿勢（+23）、顏色（+18）和接近方向（+18）上——這些因素的目標級指示並未提供指導。<lb/>總的來說，細粒度語言應該增強目標級指示：指定如何執行與要達成的目標。<lb/>項目頁面：https://finevla.xlang.ai/
2605.27284.author: Xintong Hu et.al.
2605.27284.authors: Xintong Hu, Xuhong Huang, Jinyu Zhang, Yutong Yao, Yuchong Sun, Qiuyue Wang, Mingsheng Li, Sicheng Xie, Yitao Liu, Junhao Chen, Yixuan Chen, Yingming Zheng, Shuai Bai, Tao Yu
